perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Confidentiality. The Proponent shall hold the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence, and shall not disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information to any person or entity outside of the Proponent without the prior written consent of CMHC. The Proponent shall employ such precautions as are necessary to prevent unauthorized use, access to and disclosure of Confidential Information, including but not limited to, ensuring that Confidential Information is disclosed only to those of its and its affiliates’ officers and employees who: (a) have a need to know the same; (b) have been advised of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, and the confidentiality obligations set out in this Agreement; and (c) who are bound by a similar duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Confidentiality. The Proponent shall hold the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence, and shall not disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information to any person or entity outside of the Proponent without the prior written consent of CMHC. The Proponent shall employ such precautions as are necessary to prevent unauthorized use, access to and disclosure of Confidential Information, including but not limited to, ensuring that Confidential Information is disclosed only to those of its officers, senior management, legal and IT teams whom have been advised of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, and the confidentiality obligations set out in this Agreement; and who are bound by a similar duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in Section 2.1 creates a contradiction by restricting the disclosure of Confidential Information to a select group within the Proponent's organization (officers, senior management, legal and IT). The original text allows disclosure to all of its and its affiliate's officers and employees that meet certain conditions. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the scope of permissible disclosure. Now only a few members can access the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Confidentiality. The Proponent shall hold the Confidential Information in the strictest of confidence, and shall not disclose, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information to any person or entity outside of the Proponent without the prior written consent of CMHC. The Proponent shall employ such precautions as are necessary to prevent unauthorized use, access to and disclosure of Confidential Information, including but not limited to, ensuring that Confidential Information is disclosed only to those of its officers, senior management, legal and IT teams whom have been advised of the proprietary and confidential nature of the information, and the confidentiality obligations set out in this Agreement; and who are bound by a similar duty of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.1 modifies the scope of individuals allowed to access confidential information. The original text allowed access to 'its and its affiliates’ officers and employees' meeting certain criteria. The changed text restricts this to 'officers, senior management, legal and IT teams'. This creates a direct conflict within Section 2.1 (which serves as both the changed and contradicted text) as the original broader allowance is replaced by a narrower restriction, fulfilling the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use and confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use and confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information, except that after five (5) years following the agreement's termination, the Proponent shall have no further obligation to protect the confidentiality of said information.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text in Section 4.8 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text states that confidentiality obligations 'shall survive termination of this Agreement' indefinitely. The modified text adds an exception, limiting the survival of these obligations to five years after termination. This creates an inconsistency concerning the duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.8 Continuing Obligations. The Confidential Information provided to the Proponent shall retain its confidential nature and the requirements of use and confidentiality shall survive termination of this Agreement and the return of any Confidential Information, except that after five (5) years following the agreement's termination, the Proponent shall have no further obligation to protect the confidentiality of said information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.8 introduces a time limit (five years after termination) on the confidentiality obligations, which directly contradicts the original text's intention of indefinite survival of these obligations. The original text states that the requirements 'shall survive termination of this Agreement', while the modified text limits this survival to five years. Since the changed text and the contradicted text are identical and now explicitly contradict the indefinite nature of the obligation implied in the original text, this constitutes a clear and direct in-text contradiction as defined by the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: 4.9 Assignment . This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred in whole or in part by the Proponent without CMHC’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.9 Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred in whole or in part by the Proponent without CMHC’s prior written consent, unless the Proponent undergoes a change in control due to a merger or acquisition.
perturbation.explanation: The change to section 4.9 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text prevents assignment without CMHC's consent. The modified text provides an exception for changes in control, such as mergers or acquisitions. This directly contradicts the original restriction by allowing assignment under specific circumstances without CMHC consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.9 Assignment. This Agreement may not be assigned or transferred in whole or in part by the Proponent without CMHC’s prior written consent, unless the Proponent undergoes a change in control due to a merger or acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the assignment restriction, allowing it under specific change in control scenarios, whereas the contradicted text does not contain this exception. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text and contradicted text pertain to the same rule, but have a different criteria.

perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any other agreement or discussion, oral or written.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any other agreement or discussion, oral or written. However, any pre-existing confidentiality agreements between the parties pertaining to the same subject matter shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original clause states this agreement is the 'entire agreement' and supersedes all prior agreements. The addition states pre-existing confidentiality agreements remain in effect, directly contradicting the superseding nature of the present agreement and raising legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes any other agreement or discussion, oral or written. However, any pre-existing confidentiality agreements between the parties pertaining to the same subject matter shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes any other agreement, oral or written. The changed text adds a clause stating that pre-existing confidentiality agreements remain in full force and effect. This directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause because it carves out an exception for pre-existing confidentiality agreements, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, highlighting that the new addition directly clashes with the core principle of the entire agreement clause it's contained within.
